G1: LangSmith is used for AI observability.
G2: LangGraph is a cloud storage solution.
LangGraph is used for backing up files.
LangGraph is used for syncing documents across devices.
G3: LLM-as-a-judge is completely free of bias.
G4: Trajectory evaluation scores the entire execution path of an agent.
Trajectory evaluation was introduced by OpenAI in 2023.
G5: LangSmith provides end-to-end tracing of LLM calls.
G6: LangChain has dedicated packages for Python.
LangChain has dedicated packages for Rust.
G7: LLM-as-a-judge requires a minimum calibration dataset of at least 500 human-annotated examples to work reliably.
G8: LangGraph is a graph-based agent runtime.
LangGraph is used exclusively by Fortune 500 companies.
G9: LangSmith provides over 30 built-in evaluator templates.
G10: Continual learning for AI agents refers to improving performance over time.
Continual learning is required by law for all medical AI applications.
G11: Graph models are faster than sequential pipelines.
G12: The LangSmith Developer tier is free.
The LangSmith Developer tier includes unlimited traces per month.
G13: Trajectory evaluation is useful for complex multi-step agents.
Trajectory evaluation should be run after every single LLM call in production.
G14: The harness hill-climbing recipe is an iterative process for improving AI agent performance.
The harness hill-climbing recipe typically achieves a 40 percent score improvement per cycle.
G15: Each enterprise customer pays a flat $10,000 annual fee.
G16: Context-layer continual learning involves updating instructions.
Context-layer continual learning involves updating few-shot examples.
Context-layer continual learning is the recommended starting point for teams with fewer than 10 production agents.
G17: Cisco built an agentic engineering framework using LangGraph.
The framework reduced root-cause analysis time by 93 percent.
The framework reduced costs by 50 percent.
G18: Offline evaluation uses curated datasets run in batch.
Offline evaluation is only available to LangSmith teams on the Enterprise tier.
G20: Continual learning can happen at the model layer.
Continual learning can happen at the harness layer.
Continual learning can happen at the context layer.
